items.title: We're now covering greater Austin
items.body: As of April 29 the corpus has pivoted from MO + AR to the Austin–Round Rock MSA: Travis, Williamson, Hays, Bastrop, and Caldwell counties. The MO/AR snapshot is preserved at `data/archive/2026-04-29-mo-ar/` and can be restored if we expand back. Hill Country (Burnet, Blanco, Llano) is on deck if Austin-5 inventory thins out.

items.title: Hill Country drift: where Austin homesteaders are looking
items.body: The under-$500k acreage band in Austin proper has all but vanished, but Hays + Bastrop carry a healthy supply of 5–20 acre tracts under that price. Caldwell trends cheaper still on per-acre but tilts pasture rather than woodland — know what you're optimizing for.

items.title: Buildable acreage with on-site water remains the rarest sub-corpus
items.body: Across Austin-5, listings that pair > 5 acres with an existing well, established road access, and no flood-zone overlap are roughly 7–9% of inventory. Saved-search candidates: "Austin metro, well + road, 5+ ac, < $400k".

items.title: Off-grid cabins on land are scarce in Texas
items.body: Texas listings rarely advertise 'off-grid cabin on N acres' the way Ozark inventory does — the closest equivalent is 'barndominium' or a hunting cabin tagged on raw land. Filter by Structures + No HOA + 10+ acres to find them.
